chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Mutual Inheritance of a Man and a Woman Whom He Marries and Then Divorces During His Illness

text: 5669 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَحْضُرُهُ اَلْمَوْتُ فَيُطَلِّقُ اِمْرَأَتَهُ هَلْ يَجُوزُ طَلاَقُهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ وَ هِيَ تَرِثُهُ وَ إِنْ مَاتَتْ لَمْ يَرِثْهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5669 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), who was asked about a man who is on his deathbed and divorces his wife - whether his divorce is valid.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it is valid, and she will inherit from him. However, if she dies, he does not inherit from her."
